data.title: Potential Hawkish Shift in Federal Reserve Leadership Raises Market Concerns
data.description: On January 30, Jin10 reported that Sonu Varghese, a global macro strategist at Carson Group, expressed concerns about the potential nomination of Kevin Warsh as the Federal Reserve Chair. According to Jin10, Varghese noted that Warsh has historically been hawkish, despite his recent discussions on interest rate cuts. If Warsh advocates for significant rate reductions upon joining the Federal Reserve, he may struggle to convince others of the necessity for further cuts. This scenario could lead to a deeply divided committee that refrains from implementing rate cuts altogether. In the short term, a potentially hawkish Federal Reserve could increase market volatility.

data.title: The appointment of "hawkish" Warsh to head the Federal Reserve could trigger internal divisions within the Fed and market volatility.
data.description: Sonu Varghese, global macro strategist at Carson Group, said that if Warsh is indeed nominated to chair the Federal Reserve, we may end up with a Fed that is actually somewhat hawkish. Kevin Warsh has historically been a hawk, although he has recently been talking about rate cuts. If he enters the Fed advocating for significant rate cuts, he may not have much credibility in convincing others that further rate cuts are needed. We might even end up with a deeply divided committee that doesn't cut rates at all. In the short term, a potentially hawkish Fed could increase market volatility. (Jinshi)

data.title: The Shanghai branch of the People's Bank of China: Further advance the pilot program of multilateral central bank digital currency bridges to ensure the preparation and operation of the IMF Shanghai Center.
data.description: The Shanghai Headquarters of the People's Bank of China (PBOC) held a press conference for the first quarter of 2026, introducing the financial operations in Shanghai in 2025 and answering questions from reporters. Zhou Peng, Deputy Director of the Monetary and Credit Research Department of the PBOC Shanghai Headquarters, stated that the PBOC Shanghai Headquarters has made a series of new progress in promoting the construction of Shanghai as an international financial center, and has facilitated the implementation of the policy on refinancing of blockchain-based letters of credit for shipping and trade. The International Operation Center for Digital RMB has officially commenced operations in Shanghai, and the pilot program for multilateral central bank digital currency bridges is also progressing in depth. Efforts are being made to launch the online payment function for bank cards and digital RMB hard wallets for gate access on all Shanghai rail transit lines, and full support is being provided for the preparation and operation of the International Monetary Fund (IMF) Shanghai Center.

data.title: Binance will update funding rates for TradFi perpetual contracts such as XAUUSDT.
data.description: According to the official announcement, starting from 20:15 (UTC+8) on January 30, 2026, Binance Futures will adjust the settlement frequency of funding fees for XAUUSDT, XAGUSDT, and TSLAUSDT perpetual contracts, changing it from once every four hours to once every eight hours. The upper/lower limit of the funding fee for XAUUSDT and XAGUSDT perpetual contracts will be adjusted from ±0.375% to ±0.05%.

data.title: Binance Alpha will list Kindred Labs (KIN), with an airdrop eligibility threshold of 241 points.
data.description: According to the official announcement, trading of Kindred Labs (KIN) on Binance Alpha will begin on January 30, 2026 at 19:00 (UTC+8). Users holding at least 241 Binance Alpha Points can claim a token airdrop. The airdrop of 600 KIN tokens will be available on the Alpha event page on a first-come, first-served basis. If the event continues, the point threshold will automatically decrease by 5 points every five minutes. Claiming the airdrop will consume 15 Alpha Points. Users must confirm their claim within 24 hours on the Alpha event page; otherwise, they will be considered to have forfeited their airdrop.

null
source: https://x.com/BSCNews/status/2017120776722534784

data.title: Potential Impact of Christopher Waller's Appointment on Interest Rates
data.description: Mizuho Securities' chief strategist Shoki Omori suggests that the appointment of Christopher Waller as the new Federal Reserve chair could lead to sustained pressure for interest rate cuts. According to NS3.AI, recent FOMC votes indicate that achieving these cuts may not be straightforward under Waller's leadership. The market appears to be overestimating the speed at which rate cuts will occur, likely resulting in a slower pace than anticipated. This scenario is expected to contribute to an increase in the USD/JPY exchange rate.

data.title: Senators Question Deputy Attorney General's Crypto Holdings Amid Regulatory Changes
data.description:
Six Democratic senators have raised concerns about Deputy Attorney General Todd Blanche's potential conflict of interest due to his significant cryptocurrency holdings. According to NS3.AI, Blanche is accused of easing regulatory enforcement on cryptocurrency while possessing substantial crypto assets. His recent memo led to the disbandment of the Department of Justice's National Cryptocurrency Enforcement Team and halted criminal investigations into various crypto-related activities.

The senators have pointed to an increase in illicit cryptocurrency use and are demanding transparency regarding Blanche's delayed divestment from his crypto holdings. They are also seeking clarity on his communications with the cryptocurrency industry. The situation has sparked a debate over the ethical implications of holding crypto assets while influencing regulatory policies.

data.title: Data: The market capitalization of USD1 surpasses $5 billion, setting a new record.
data.description: According to Coingecko data, the market capitalization of USD1, a stablecoin launched by WLFI, a crypto project of the Trump family, has exceeded $5 billion, currently trading at $5,049,275,677, setting a new record. It has also surpassed DAI to become the fifth largest stablecoin by market capitalization, after USDT, USDC, USDS, and USDE.

data.title: A whale spent 6 million USDC to buy 1108.79 XAUT, with an average purchase price of $5411.
data.description: According to Onchain Lens monitoring, a whale spent 6 million USDC to buy 1108.79 XAUT tokens at an average price of $5411. The whale then deposited these tokens into Aave V3 for future use. Currently, the whale still holds 12.58 million USDC and may withdraw funds to buy more XAUT.

data.title: Mizuho Bank: If Warsh is elected Fed Chairman, the market will feel continued pressure to cut interest rates.
data.description: Shoki Omori, chief strategist at Mizuho Securities, stated that if Warsh is elected as the new Federal Reserve Chairman, the market will feel continued pressure to cut interest rates. However, considering the voting results at the last FOMC meeting, cutting rates will not be easy for him. The market misjudged the pace of rate cuts, expecting the Fed to cut rates more slowly than the market anticipated or hoped for. At the same time, the market perceives Warsh as more dovish than Hassett, which is why we are seeing the dollar rise against the yen. (Jinshi)

data.title: Circle: Arc will gradually move from the testnet to the production environment, with a focus on building resilient stablecoin infrastructure this year.
data.description: Stablecoin issuer Circle announced it will focus on building a more resilient stablecoin infrastructure in 2026 to drive further adoption at the enterprise and institutional levels. Circle's Chief Product and Technology Officer, Nikhil Chandhok, revealed plans to gradually move its Layer 1 blockchain, Arc, aimed at institutions and large-scale applications, from the testnet to production. Simultaneously, Circle will continue to expand the use cases for stablecoins issued by USDC, EURC, USYC, and its partners, improving token availability and reach by supporting more blockchain networks. Chandhok stated that this includes deepening native support on key networks, strengthening integration with Arc, and making it easier for institutional users to hold, transfer, and programmatically use stablecoins, integrating them into their daily business processes. (Cointelegraph)

data.title: Understanding Bitcoin's Growth Patterns: Power Law vs. Parabola
data.description: Plan C posted on X. The discussion highlights the distinction between a power law and a parabola in the context of financial growth patterns. A power law is characterized as sustainable growth over an extended period, whereas a parabola represents exponential growth within a condensed timeframe. The article notes that gold has not experienced a parabolic trend over the past decade, but rather only recently. In contrast, Bitcoin's growth is described as high yet sustained over a longer duration, adhering to a power law. Historically, Bitcoin has experienced several parabolic surges near major peaks, which often precede bear markets, indicating the unsustainable nature of such rallies.

data.title: Bitcoin Declines as Kevin Warsh's Fed Chair Prospects Rise
data.description:
Bitcoin's value has dropped significantly, nearing $81,000, amid increasing speculation that Kevin Warsh may become the next Federal Reserve Chair. According to Odaily, Markus Thielen, founder of 10x Research, noted that the market views Warsh's potential appointment as a negative factor for Bitcoin. This is due to Warsh's emphasis on monetary discipline, higher real interest rates, and tighter liquidity, which align with his policy framework that considers crypto assets as speculative products of a loose monetary environment rather than tools to hedge against currency devaluation.

Higher real interest rates, which reflect the true cost of financing after inflation, typically suppress demand for risk assets, including Bitcoin. Additionally, several observers believe that Warsh's hawkish stance and underestimation of deflation risks may have exacerbated economic downturn pressures in the past. If similar policies are adopted, it could lead to higher unemployment, slower recovery, and increased deflation risks.

data.title: The rising probability of Kevin Warsh becoming Federal Reserve Chairman has fueled market concerns that a "return to monetary discipline" could negatively impact Bitcoin.
data.description: Bitcoin fell further, nearing $81,000, as prediction markets surged on the probability of Kevin Warsh becoming the next Federal Reserve Chairman. Markus Thielen, founder of 10x Research, pointed out that the market generally views Warsh's regained policy influence as a negative factor for Bitcoin. This is because he has long emphasized monetary discipline, higher real interest rates, and tighter liquidity, and his policy framework tends to view crypto assets as "speculative products of a loose monetary environment" rather than tools for hedging against currency depreciation. Higher real interest rates mean that the real cost of financing increases after adjusting for inflation, which typically suppresses demand for risk assets, including Bitcoin. Furthermore, many observers believe that his hawkish stance and underestimation of deflation risks may have exacerbated downward economic pressures in the past; continuing with a similar approach could lead to higher unemployment, a slower recovery, and a greater risk of deflation. (CoinDesk)

data.title: Bitget stock contracts will be upgraded to a 24/7 trading mode.
data.description: According to an official announcement, Bitget stock contracts will be upgraded to a 24/7 uninterrupted trading mode on February 7, 2026. After the upgrade, trading will no longer be affected by weekends or public holidays. Furthermore, to ensure that the contract market price is anchored to the traditional market price, the mark price will be limited to a range of ±3% of the index price, which will be composed of traditional market prices. This upgrade aims to provide a more flexible and efficient trading experience, allowing users to reasonably configure stop-loss and take-profit orders according to their own risk tolerance.

data.title: US DOJ Finalizes $400M Forfeiture Linked to Helix Crypto Mixer
data.description:
The US Department of Justice has completed the forfeiture of more than $400 million in assets tied to Helix.
source: https://cryptonews.com/news/us-doj-finalizes-400m-forfeiture-linked-to-helix-crypto-mixer/

data.title: USD/JPY Surpasses 154 with Daily Increase of 0.61%
data.description: The USD/JPY exchange rate has risen above 154, marking a daily increase of 0.61%. According to ChainCatcher, this development reflects ongoing fluctuations in the currency market. The rise in the exchange rate highlights the dynamic nature of currency trading and the factors influencing the valuation of the U.S. dollar against the Japanese yen.

data.title: Analysts predict that Bitcoin will continue to weaken relative to US stocks, and a short-term rotation of funds is unlikely.
data.description: Crypto analyst Benjamin Cowen stated that Bitcoin's current downtrend may not end as quickly as some holders expect. "Bitcoin will likely continue to underperform relative to the stock market," Cowen pointed out in a video on Thursday. He believes market expectations of a large-scale rotation of funds from precious metals like gold and silver to crypto assets may be overly optimistic. Precious metal prices have been consistently strong recently. Data shows that gold and silver prices have risen to all-time highs of $5,608.33 and $121.64 respectively. Citigroup further predicts that silver may rise to $150 in the next three months, driven by Chinese demand and a four-year low for the dollar. However, Cowen believes that a short-term rotation of funds to Bitcoin is "unlikely to happen." While many in the crypto market believe that new highs in precious metals often foreshadow a subsequent Bitcoin rally, he emphasizes that the current cyclical structure does not support this assessment. As of press time, Bitcoin was trading at $82,859, down 7.78% in the past seven days. Meanwhile, market sentiment continued to weaken, with the crypto fear and greed index falling to 16, placing it in the "extreme fear" zone. (CoinTelegraph)

data.title: Cardano to Integrate Privacy-Enhanced USDCx Stablecoin
data.description: Cardano founder Charles Hoskinson announced that the blockchain will soon support USDCx, a privacy-focused version of the USDC stablecoin. According to NS3.AI, USDCx utilizes Zero-Knowledge Proofs technology to encrypt transaction details, ensuring that the sender, receiver, and transaction amount remain concealed from external observers. This development aims to bolster privacy for stablecoin transactions on Cardano's network.
